sections.paragraph:
Ἄραβοι Arabs Ac 2:11 D (for Ἄραβες) could have been wrongly formed fr. the gen. pl. Ἀράβων (Appian, Syr. 51 §257 ἐξ Ἀράβων; Ps 71:10; EpArist 114; Jos., Ant. 2, 213; Ἀρράβων Just., D. 34, 4; 119, 4), perh. as ἅλα fr. ἅλατος (s. ἅλας beg.).

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. fr. = from

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. pl. = plural

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

perh. perh. = perhaps

beg. beg. = beginning

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 128
